# Anchored Spec

Anchored Spec is a Backstage-aligned, spec-as-source architecture framework for repositories that want entities, relations, drift detection, traceability, reporting, and AI-ready context.

This file is the machine-oriented starting point for AI agents. Prefer the linked guides over inferring behavior from scattered prose.

## Use Anchored Spec Correctly

- Treat Backstage-style entity YAML or Markdown frontmatter as the source of truth.
- Preserve the repository's current storage mode: manifest mode or inline mode.
- Prefer Backstage built-in kinds before custom anchored-spec kinds.
- Use canonical entity refs such as `component:default/orders-service`.
- Keep docs, relations, and validation in sync with the entity graph.
- Treat discovery output as draft or observed input, not silent source-of-truth replacement.
- Re-run validation after meaningful model changes.
- Use trace, drift, diff, reconcile, and context workflows instead of ad-hoc repo guessing.

## Agent Guides

- [Expanded LLM guide](./llms-full.txt)
- [Agent guide](./docs/llms/agent-guide.md)

- [Docs and traceability](./docs/information/docs-and-traceability.md)
- [Testing and CI](./docs/delivery/testing-and-ci.md)

## Best-Fit Tasks

- model a system, API, resource, ownership group, or architectural decision
- add or update entity relations and linked docs
- discover draft entities from OpenAPI, Kubernetes, Terraform, SQL DDL, dbt, Markdown, anchors, or tree-sitter
- analyze drift between declared and observed architecture
- generate review artifacts such as graphs, diagrams, reports, impact, constraints, and context bundles
- evaluate compatibility and policy impacts for changes
